title: About
text:
Consultant economic analyst. I work with public data, statistical modeling, and spatial analysis to build arguments about policy and markets.
Tools: R, SQL, Quarto, Leaflet, tidycensus, sf
Interests: Urban economics, food systems, labor markets, public finance
GitHub

title: TN Williams Goes to Washington
text: Welcome. I’m a recovering consultant who likes coding/writing. This site is where I publish analysis, maps, and the occasional polemic backed by numbers.

title: TN Williams Goes to Washington
text:
What’s Here

Blog — Short-form analysis and data explorations
Projects — Longer-form interactive work (maps, dashboards, models)

title: TN Williams Goes to Washington
text:
Current Focus
DC Food Access & Urban Heat — Mapping the intersection of food deserts, extreme heat, transit gaps, and the economic shock of federal workforce reductions on DC’s restaurant and retail landscape.
Built with Quarto and R. Published on GitHub Pages.

title: DC Food Access, Heat, and the Federal Workforce
text:
An interactive exploration of food access in Washington, DC — layering grocery store locations, store hours, urban heat islands, transit coverage, demographics, and the downstream effects of federal workforce reductions on DC’s food and restaurant landscape.
Work in progress.
